methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $44M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Supersonic & Hypersonic (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 2 same-sector peers exist in Supersonic & Hypersonic, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Boom Supersonic
private.rationale: Same sector (Supersonic & Hypersonic), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.5B

data.narrative:
Venus Aerospace, a Series B-stage supersonic and hypersonic developer founded by Sassie and Andrew Duggleby, has raised $44M+ to date. The sector comprises a limited peer universe of two public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and two private competitors (Boom Supersonic, Hermeus), reflecting the nascent and capital-intensive nature of frontier aerospace.

Given the narrow and heterogeneous peer set in Supersonic & Hypersonic—only two same-sector comparables exist, with insufficient same-stage or valuation-tier matches—a peer-median multiple approach is not reliable. Instead, the valuation rests on stage-progression methodology. Using $44M in total capital raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B to subsequent-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $84M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $71M to $96M, with a median point of $84M. This range reflects sensitivity around the stage-progression step-up multiplier and the inherent uncertainty in applying standardized progression ratios to a frontier subsector with limited public comparables and heterogeneous business models. Key uncertainties include technology maturity, regulatory pathway clarity, and capital efficiency relative to the sparse peer set. A second valuation method triangulation would strengthen conviction, pending access to more granular peer financials or transaction comps.
